turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I just had a terrible week.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What a shame!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I fell down and hurt my arm.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: That's too bad.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Then my son drove into a tree.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I'm sorry to hear that.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: The dog bit the delivery boy.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What a pity!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: And a storm blew our roof away.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Ho, no!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hi Mark.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Hi.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What are you planning to do today?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I'm not sure yet.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Would you like to have lunch with me?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes. When?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Is 11:30 AM OK?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Sorry, I didn't hear you. Can you say that again please?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I said , 11:30 AM.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Oh, I'm busy then. Can we meet a little later?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: How may I help you?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I want to return this video.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Is that all you need?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I also need to check this magazine out.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: You're not allowed to do that.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I don't understand.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: It's our policy.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What can I check out?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Only books or videos.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I need this magazine.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Have you seen that house?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, it looks interesting.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: You're right. I love it very much.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It must be old.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: At least more than 100 years old.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Let's enter it!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I can't open the door.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It must be locked.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Let me ask the old woman in that reception office.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: She can't answer you.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: How may I help you?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I would like to return this book.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Is that all you need?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I also want to check out this video.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Is that all you would like to check out?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: That's all I need.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Do you have your library card?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Here it is.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Make sure to take proper care of this video.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: That won't be a problem.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Ah, ah, ah ...

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: All right, Bill. Here' s your daily exercise schedule. You are to jog before breakfast.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Jog?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Then, you are to walk to work.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Walk?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Thirty minutes in gym at lunch time.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Oh no.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Use the stairs, never the elevator.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Oh, dear.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: And three times a week, you can either swim, play racketball, or hand ball.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What do you need?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I need to use the Internet.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: You have your library card, right?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, I do.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: There is a wait right now to use the computers.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: That's fine.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Would you please write your name on this list?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Then what?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I will call you when a computer is free.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: How do I log on to the computer?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: May I help you?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What time will the library be closing?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: It closes at six.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Does it always close at six?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: No, not every day.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Will the library be open on Saturday?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Absolutely.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What are your hours on Saturday?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Saturday's hours are from nine in the morning to six-thirty at night.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: All right. Thank you.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Do you want black or white coffee?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: White, please

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Look! there's a Folk concert tomorrow evening. Do you Want to go?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I don't like Folk music very much.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What kind of music do you like?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I like classical music. Do you?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Not very much. Classical music sends me to sleep

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I don't believe you.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: It's true.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: You are funny!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Are you new to this school?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, I'm new.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Is this your first year in college?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I transferred from another school.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Where did you transfer from?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I was at PCC before.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Why did you transfer?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I was going to go here first, but my grades weren't good enough.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Did you like it at PCC?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: PCC is an incredible school.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What can I do for you?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I am trying to find this book.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What's the problem?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It doesn't seem like this library has it.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Have you checked the computer?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I have already.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What did it say?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It says the book is on the shelf, but I didn't find it there.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I can always borrow the book from another library.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: You can do that?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Who's singing in your home, Jim?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It's Ellie and her friends.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Who's Ellie?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: She's my little niece. It' s her birthday. They' re singing``Happy Birthday ``.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Oh, I see. how old is she?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: She's ten.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I suppose they're playing games.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Oh, yes. Ellie was playing games all day yesterday. By the way, Alice, when's your birthday?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Today.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Today?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Do you know where I can park?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Do you need a spot here or off campus?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I want a space on campus.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: There's a parking structure for students.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Where can I find it?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Go to the western side of the campus, and you'll find it there.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Is it full?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: When I went up there, it wasn't full at all.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: When was that?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Sometime this morning.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hi, Charles. What's the matter?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Oh! ! My car isn't working. I'm waiting for a tow-truck.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Do you have a ride home?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, my wife is coming to get me.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: That's good. Do you live near here?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: No, we live in the suburbs. What about you?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I live downtown, with my parents.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Do you have a car?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I don't need a car. I walk to work.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: You're lucky!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hurry up, Daniel, it's Barbie time.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: All right, Sis!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Come on, it's cool outside.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I really wish Mom and Dad were here with us.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hey, Daniel, what's your favorite weather? Snowy or sunny?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Me? Er, I like hot weather, just like today.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What? Are you crazy?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I like to watch Barbie sticking her tongue out.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Ew, Danny, gross!

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: By the way, what's your favorite weather?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: How may I help you?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I need to get on the Internet.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Do you have your library card with you?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, I have it.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: You're going to have to wait for a computer.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: That's okay.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Please put your name on this list.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Then what do I need to do?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: When there is a computer available, I will call you.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What do I do to log on to the computer?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Do you want to go to the beach tonight?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Sure, which beach are we going to?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I wanted to go to Malibu beach.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I like Santa Monica.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: The water there looks so dirty.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I just like the pier they have at Santa Monica.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I like the pier too, because it's beautiful at night.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Is Malibu beach nice?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: It's a lot nicer, and I really like it better.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: So you want to go to Malibu?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Mumm. Those burgers smell great. It's perfect day for a barbecue.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It sure is. I'm glad it didn't rain. My name's Mike Gates, by the way.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Oh, hi! I'm Barbara Johnson. Nice to meet you.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I'm sorry. What's your name again?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Barbara. But please, just call me Barb.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: So Barb... what do you do?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I'm studying medicine.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Really? Where?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: At Harvard. What about you?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I'm an engineer at IBM.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Why didn't you tell me you had a girlfriend?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Sorry, I thought you knew.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: But you should have told me you were in love with her.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Didn't I?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: You know you didn't.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Well, I'm telling you now.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes, but you might have told me before.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I didn't think you'd be interested.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: You can't be serious. How dare you not tell me you were going to marry her?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Sorry, I didn't think it mattered.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Could you tell me what university you went to, Mr. Smith?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I went to Yale University.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Did you? And what did you study?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I have a B. A. in Economics.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Would you mind telling me how old you are?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I'm twenty-seven.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: And could you tell me what other jobs you have had?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, I have worked in a bank for the last years.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: And what was your salary at the bank?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I got $500 a week.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: David, why don't you clean your room?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I am not in the mood.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Why are you feeling depress?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I was told my girlfriend was speaking ill of me. It's a real letdown.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I don't think she would do such a thing.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: But she did and it made me disappointed.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Oh, cheer up! A girlfriend is not everything.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: But she means a lot to me.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Then forgive her mistake.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Oh, I just can't forget it.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Why don't you sit down and relax, darling?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I don't want to.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Well, come and talk to me then.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Certainly not.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: May I turn on the radio then?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Turn on the radio? What for?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: So that we can sit down together and listen to some music.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Listen to some music? And who'll cook dinner? Will you?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Ok, I will. But let's go to a disco after dinner.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: To a disco? Oh, no. You know I hate pop.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: You're not looking happy. What' s the matter?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Oh, nothing special. I'm just a bit tired.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: With the job?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: With everything, with everybody, with all this!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: A good suggestion for you. You need a holiday.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It wasn't always like this, you know.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What do you mean?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Well, I mean. We always do the same thing. There' s no variety in our lives.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: You need a holiday. That' s what's the matter.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Certainly, perhaps.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Are you going to the party on Saturday?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I was thinking about it. Are you?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yeah, I heard it's going to be a lot of fun.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Really? Well, what time does it start?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: It starts at 8

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Well, who all is going to be there?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Everyone from school.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: How do you know it's going to be so fun?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: This party is going to have a DJ, food, and drinks.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Wow, that does sound like it's going to be fun.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Could you tell me what university you want to go to, John?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Pardon?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What university would you enter?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: University? Why? You asked me last year.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Oh, I forget. Sorry.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I went to Harvard University.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Did you? And what course did you take there?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: God save me! Is there anything wrong with you? Didn't I tell you?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I've not known. Perhaps, I've got a bad memory.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I did a B. A. in economics. Remember?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Could you tell me what university you want to go to, John?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Pardon?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What university would you enter?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: University? Why? You asked me last year.

turns.english: Oh, I forget. Sorry.

turns.english: I went to Harvard University.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Did you? And what course did you take there?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: God save me! Is there anything wrong with you? Didn't I tell you?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I've not known. Perhaps, I've got a bad memory.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I did a B. A. in economics. Remember?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Would you like to stretch your legs?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Sure.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Let's get a soft drink.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Do we have enough time?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: It's a fifteen-minute intermission.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: The seats are excellent. We're right in the center.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: It's a new concert hall and the acoustics are great.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I can't agree more.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Is this your first time to come to a symphony concert?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: In the United States, yes. But back in China, I went to concerts a couple of times with my parents.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hi Rose, what are you busy with right now?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Hi Jack, I'm working on these documents. The manager wants them for half an hour.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Well, Rose.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Is there something any need?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Are your free this weekend?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, I have nothing to do.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Great, Is it convenient if i visit you this weekend?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I beg your pardon?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I'd like to call on you this weekend. I just want to a drop in for a chat.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Really? well, ok, you're welcome.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hey, Vernassa. why be angry look?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It's the new neighbor. I hate her guts.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I've never seen you so worked up. what did she do to you?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: She made another white crack about my clothes.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Are you sure she was trying to put you down?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Of course, I am sure.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: You shall go easy on her. she is new here.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Just how am I supposed to do that?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: You shall just take it in surprise.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I've tried, but she keeps pushing my buttons.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What's the matter with you then? You look miserable.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It's us.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What do you mean by us.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Well, you always say you're busy.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: That's right.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: And you often go back to live with your parents and leave our son in the room by himself.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I ... I ... I miss my parents, also they miss me.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Oh I remember, I cut terrible calls, and you didn't say anything about it.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: You mean I am groaned a few words?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Totally not. Perhaps it's about our marriage.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I would like to register for a class today.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: No problem, what class would you like to take?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I would very much enjoy taking a Psychology class. Because I'm crazy.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: There are two classes that are still open.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Which days are these classes on?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: The first class is a Tuesday and Thursday class from two to three.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What about the other class?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: The other class is on Monday and Wednesday from 10 am - 12.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Are you sure there are no more open classes?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I'm positive.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Professor, I am going to need to leave class early today.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What is the problem?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I need to go to the airport to pick up a relative.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Are you planning on having someone take notes for you?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes, I've got that part covered.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: When, exactly do you need to leave?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I don't need to leave until the very end of class.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Fine, but next time try and not have to miss class again.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Thanks, Professor. See you later!

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: See you later! Drive carefully.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What do you do when you are angry?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I usually calm down first and think about the reason that caused my anger.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: That's smart. What do you usually do to relieve your anger?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Something like listening to music is a good idea.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Oh, what kind of music do you listen to?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Classic. Something that could calm your mind.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I think that might work for me as well.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Working out is another good way to relieve anger.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Like what?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Jogging, yoga, or even just taking a walk.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hello! I'm Penny, I moved here just now.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Oh, Penny, I'm Leonard, glad to meet you.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Nice to meet you, too. I thought I should come and say hello to you. I haven't known anybody.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It is hard to get used to a new place, isn't it?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes. Would you like to come here later to have a coffee? Bring all your family.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: We'd like to. Can you tell me when we go?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: About 4.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: OK. Do you want us to bring something?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: No, just come here.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: OK, see you later.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Do you like flowers?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Of course, I like.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What's your favorite flower?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Forsythia. It's also called winter jasmine which is the symbol of the spring's arrival.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Spring is a lively season.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes. How about you?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I admire plum blossoms very much. It seems that only it can blossom in the cold winter.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: You have a perfect taste!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: It is the symbol of laughing at hoar frost and fighting with snow. I admire the bravery of plum blossoms.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: But it used to be ignored by many people.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Wow! I can't believe I'm standing here!

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What are you so excited about?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I'm standing in front of an exquisite work of art!

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What's the big deal? It's just a picture of some woman.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I've seen it so many times in books, but I never imagined I'd get to see it in person.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: There are tons of other paintings that I think are better.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Maybe so, but do you have any idea how much this one is worth?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: No, how much is it worth?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: About 80 million dollars.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Whoa.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: The band is pretty good. How do you like the music?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It's very nice. I haven't heard live music in a while. This is fun.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Well, then, may I invite you for the next dance?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Of course. But I'm afraid I'm not much of a dancer ...

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Don't worry. I'm not much of a dancer myself.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: You're dancing so well.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: You dance beautifully too.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: When did you learn to dance?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: In college. But I don't dance very often. What's your favourite dance?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It's hard to say. It depends. But I love the waltz.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Here's your hot dog and beer. What happened? Did I miss anything?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yeah, Cal Ripen just hit a home run.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What's the score?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Well it was 3 to 4, but Ripen's home run made it 5 to 4 since another player was on first base.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: So Baltimore is winning?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Right.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: This is a really great place to watch a baseball game.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yeah, there isn't a bad seat in the place.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: The fans are great here, too. Not like in Philadelphia.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It was a great idea to spend a day watching a game.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Oh, my God! I've been cheated!

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What? What did you buy?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: It's a brick! I can't believe how stupid I was. Damn it!

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What is it? Why did you buy a brick?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: There was a guy on the sidewalk. He had three new boxes, Panasonic video camera boxes. He said he had to get rid of them quick.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: And?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: So he said he'd sell me one for twenty dollars. A four-hundred-dollar camera for twenty bucks.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: And it's a brick?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I can't believe how stupid you are. Why didn't you open the box?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Are you coming to the bridal shower for Jane?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, I am. When is the wedding?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Aug 8.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: August bride. How nice! By the way, who is going to be the matron of honor?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Mrs. White.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Oh, yeah? I wonder who is going to give Jane away at the wedding. You know her father passed away several years ago.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Maybe her uncle will.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I think so. The wedding will be held at her church, right?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Right. Red Rochester will officiate at the wedding.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Do you know where they will go on their honeymoon?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Come back. You haven't paid yet.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: May, just let him go.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: But he took some newspaper away without paying.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I know.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Why? You make me confused.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: He lives nearby and he would just glance at the newspaper headlines, read the political sections and return them later.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Why does he have such a privilege?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: He used to be a hero during the war time and everyone respect him.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I see. Does he live by himself?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, he has no children and his wife passed away when he was in the battle.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Well, I am a regular member of this club.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Do you have to pay to join?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes, membership fees vary depending on the club but usually they run into tens of thousands of dollars.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Wow, that much? Don't they have any public courses?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Very few, unfortunately.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: How was your game today?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Extremely good. I shot a 78, including 5 birdies!

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Congratulations! What's your normal handicap?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Ten. How did your game go?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: My drives were terrible today. I kept slicing the ball into the roughs and sand traps.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: what a nice garden! Look at these tulips!

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: that would be a good shot. Can you take my picture here, dear? Please try to get the garden in the frame as well.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: all right. Would you please stand closer to the flower bed? Good. Smile. Say``cheese ``. OK, got you. That's terrific.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: thanks. Could you take one more from this angle?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: no problem.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: now let's have one together.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: do you know how to set the timer?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: let me see... set the timer first and push the button, oh, come here.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: OK, let's have a try.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: cheese ...

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Kathy! How are you? It's been ages! What are you doing these days?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I just opened my own restaurant. I'm also head chef.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: You're kidding! You've always hated cooking!

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Well, I used to hate cooking, but now I love it.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: When did you change your mind?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: After I tried French food. Before that, I used to cook really boring things for my family.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I still can't believe it! So, did you go to cooking school or something?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yeah. I went to California to study.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Really? How long did you stay there?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I was there between 88 and 90.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hi, Martin. I'Ve been looking for you all morning.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What's up?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I want to say goodbye. I'm leaving for America tomorrow.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: America? Did you get admit to MIT?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Congratulations! Susan. I'm so happy for you.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Thank you.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Really going to miss you.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Me too. I'll write to you once I get settled. But I have so much to do. You might have to wait a while. But I really will write to you. I love to write letters. I think it's more meaning than the emails even than the phone call.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Ok. Take care. Have a nice journey.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I've got some bad news about the bike you lent me.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What's that?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I fell on the way to school, and your bike got scratched. I'm really sorry.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Don't worry about it. It's not new, it already has a few scratches. Did you get hurt?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: No, thank you.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: That's the most important thing.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: It's kind of you to say. I feel a little stupid.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Forget about it.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: When you lent me the bike, it looked brand new, almost anyway.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Maybe, but really I have fallen a couple of times and it's been hit once or twice as well.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Spring Festival is the most important festival in China, isn't it?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, that's true.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: How long is the holiday?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It depends. But usually it's about 15 days.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Do you eat any special food for the festival?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, dumpling is a must. There is also other food. For example, people eat chicken for good luck.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: That's interesting. I'm told the Chinese firework is very beautiful, isn't it?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, it's really wonderful!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Oh, great! I hope I can spend this Spring Festival with you here in Beijing.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I'm sure you'll have a good time.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Adam, could you show me around the school?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: No problem.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What's the tallest building?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: You mean the white building near the playground?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: That is the library. And it has more than 1,000 , 000 books.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What's the building to the south of the library?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: You know, our school is divided into two parts, the junior high school and the senior high school. That is the new classroom building for our senior high school.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Is there a swimming pool in your school?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes. There is a large swimming pool, but it is only available in summer.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hey, Mark, long time no see.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Julie! It's good to see you again. How have you been?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Great. I just got back from a week's vacation in Thailand.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Really? How was it?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Fantastic! I didn't want to come back.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I hear the beaches in Thailand are beautiful.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: They are. And not only that the people are friendly, but also the food is delicious, not to mention cheap.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I do like Thai food. Did you see much of Bangkok?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Not much. I've got enough city life in Beijing.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I know what you mean. I could use a vacation myself.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: you look better today. How did your test go?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: much better than it did yesterday.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: did you pass?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I not only passed my test, but I aced it! I'm so happy!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: you should be. You worked really hard last night preparing for it.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: thanks for helping me with it. If you hadn't encouraged me to do my best, I wouldn't have ever been able to pass.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: you don't have to thank me. It's just a part of my job as your counsellor.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: did you always do well at school?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: no, in fact, I was terrible at taking exams.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: really?
